insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change provides more flexibility and control over the data fetched from the API. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The GraphQL query should include the @inContext directive with the channelId argument, for example, @inContext(channelId: 'online-store').
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and relevant data being displayed in their storefronts, as developers can now fetch data specific to their sales channels.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over the data fetched from the Storefront API, allowing developers to build more customized and relevant storefronts for merchants.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for apps storing cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: App Store requirements have been updated to include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports an optional channelId argument for applying sales channel context to queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: Fields productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle have been added to ExchangeLineItem.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and preserves actionable build errors during h2 deploy.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects.

updates.title: skeleton@2026.4.5
updates.summary: skeleton@2026.4.5 adds an explicit ~ app alias to new Hydrogen projects.

updates.title: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target
updates.summary: POS Extensions now supports a background extension target, allowing extensions to observe POS events and run background logic without rendering UI.

recap.headline: Shopify updates require mTLS certificates, new Hydrogen features, and App Store requirement changes

themes.title: Security Updates
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps storing cardholder data must comply. Additionally, the `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` packages have been updated with patch changes.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI Updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released with features such as non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and explicit `~ app` aliases in new Hydrogen projects. The CLI has also been updated with new store commands.

themes.title: API and App Store Changes
themes.detail: The Storefront API now supports `channelId` in the `@inContext` directive, and new fields have been added to the `ExchangeLineItem` type. The App Store has also updated its requirements, including the use of unique app names.

actions.text: Update apps to use mTLS certificates for card deposit endpoint
Review and comply with updated App Store requirements
Test and update apps with new Storefront API features
